docs.text: About Me My name is Cameron Furbush. I have experience working as a Site Reliability Engineer in both large and small companies. I received a Bachelor's degree in Information Technology from the University of West Florida. When I'm not working or focusing on technology, my time has recently been focused on training for a triathlon.

docs.text: What is YADM? YADM stands for Yet Another Dotfiles Manager, this allows you to track dotfiles using git. Once you run yadm clone locally, this will automatically distribute your dotfiles on your machine and update them with any changes. Setup Install yadm sh apt update apt install yadm or sh brew install yadm Clone the repo using yadm sh yadm clone git@github.com:Camfurbush/dotfiles.git Update when needed sh yadm pull origin main Troubleshooting How to encrypt files sh export GPG_TTY=$(tty) # Add this to your ~/.bashrc yadm encrypt How to add files using yadm sh yadm add ~/.config/yadm/files.gpg yadm commit -m "add encrypted files" yadm push origin HEAD:main How to update files locally sh yadm pull origin main
docs.title: What is YADM?

docs.text: Add Discord Webhook To create a Discord webhook, follow these steps: Open Discord and navigate to the server where you want to create the webhook. Click on the server settings menu (the gear icon next to the server name) and select "Integrations". Click on the "Create Webhook" button and enter a name for your webhook. Customize the avatar and username for your webhook if desired. Copy the webhook URL and save it for later use. Click on the "Save" button to create your webhook. You can now use the webhook URL to send messages to your Discord server from external applications or services.
docs.title: Add Discord Webhook

Click on the server settings menu (the gear icon next to the server name) and select "Integrations". Click on the "Create Webhook" button and enter a name for your webhook. Customize the avatar and username for your webhook if desired. Copy the webhook URL and save it for later use. Click on the "Save" button to create your webhook. You can now use the webhook URL to send messages to your Discord server from external applications or services.
docs.title: Add Discord Webhook

docs.text: Find Large Files Linux To find large files on a Linux system, use the following command: du -a /opt | sort -n -r | head -n 15 | numfmt --to=iec --format="%-5f"
docs.title: Find Large Files Linux

docs.text: Add Swap The steps below will walk an operator through adding a 4gb swap space to a node Steps Run fallocate -l 4G /swapfile Run dd if=/dev/zero of=/swapfile bs=1024 count=4194304 Run chmod 600 /swapfile Run mkswap /swapfile Run swapon /swapfile Run vim /etc/fstab Run /swapfile swap swap defaults 0 0
docs.title: Add Swap

docs.text: The steps below will walk an operator through adding a 4gb swap space to a node

docs.text: Devcontainers Devcontainers are a feature of Visual Studio Code that enables developers to develop in a containerized environment. Here are the steps to get started with Devcontainers: Install Visual Studio Code Install the Remote Development extension Open a project in Visual Studio Code Click on the green "><" icon in the bottom left corner of the window Select "Remote-Containers: Reopen in Container" Wait for the container to build and start Develop as usual within the container Devcontainers are useful because they enable developers to work in an isolated environment with all the necessary tools and dependencies pre-installed. This can be especially helpful when working with complex applications or in a team setting.
docs.title: Devcontainers

docs.text: Expand Disk Expand disk in vSphere Restart VM fdisk /dev/sda Enter p to print your initial partition table. Enter d (delete) followed by 2 to delete the existing partition definition (partition 1 is usually /boot and partition 2 is usually the root partition). Enter n (new) followed by p (primary) followed by 2 to re-create partition number 2 and enter to accept the start block and enter again to accept the end block which is defaulted to the end of the disk. Enter p to print your new partition table and make sure the start block matches what was in the initial partition table printed above. Enter w to write the partition table to disk. You will see an error about Device or resource busy which you can ignore. partx -a /dev/sda xfs_growfs /
docs.title: Expand Disk

docs.text: Expand disk in vSphere Restart VM fdisk /dev/sda Enter p to print your initial partition table. Enter d (delete) followed by 2 to delete the existing partition definition (partition 1 is usually /boot and partition 2 is usually the root partition). Enter n (new) followed by p (primary) followed by 2 to re-create partition number 2 and enter to accept the start block and enter again to accept the end block which is defaulted to the end of the disk. Enter p to print your new partition table and make sure the start block matches what was in the initial partition table printed above. Enter w to write the partition table to disk. You will see an error about Device or resource busy which you can ignore. partx -a /dev/sda xfs_growfs /
docs.title: Expand Disk

docs.text: Gitlab Gotchas Multi-line variables must have /n for new lines and then have echo -e $VAR to interpolate them properly Setup Gitlab Runners Run sudo vim /srv/gitlab-runner/config/config.toml to edit the config file Run the following to create a gitlab-runner sudo docker run -d --name gitlab-runner1 --restart always \ -v /srv/gitlab-runner/config:/etc/gitlab-runner \ -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \ gitlab/gitlab-runner:latest Run sudo docker run --rm -it -v /srv/gitlab-runner/config:/etc/gitlab-runner gitlab/gitlab-runner register
docs.title: Gitlab

docs.text: Multi-line variables must have /n for new lines and then have echo -e $VAR to interpolate them properly
docs.title: Gotchas

docs.text: Run sudo vim /srv/gitlab-runner/config/config.toml to edit the config file Run the following to create a gitlab-runner sudo docker run -d --name gitlab-runner1 --restart always \ -v /srv/gitlab-runner/config:/etc/gitlab-runner \ -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \ gitlab/gitlab-runner:latest Run sudo docker run --rm -it -v /srv/gitlab-runner/config:/etc/gitlab-runner gitlab/gitlab-runner register

docs.text: NFS Client Setup Walks through how to connect to an NFS share Requirements Must have NFS client for Windows enabled to interact with the NFS server on truenas.lan Steps Run mount -o anon \\truenas.lan\mnt\Pool1 Z: Open regedit and edit HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\ClientForNFS\CurrentVersion\Default Add New DWORD(32-bit) value inside of default for AnonymousUid and AnonymousGid Restart the your machine Run mount -o nolock -o anon \\truenas.lan\mnt\Pool1\NFS Z:
docs.title: NFS Client Setup

docs.text: Setup Install Ansible Steps for installing ansible sudo apt install -y software-properties-common sudo add-apt-repository --yes --update ppa:ansible/ansible sudo apt update sudo apt install -y ansible
docs.title: Setup
